blockedAssertions.blockedBy: No seeded returned-task record is visible to the local designer/reviewer UI identities, so cross-surface returned-task semantics cannot be exercised through the running app.
The live environment does not expose a reviewer-returned task that later appears in the designer returned-task flow for the same task id.
Canonical returned-task rule cannot be verified on the real UI without at least one returned/resubmittable task rendered in designer surfaces.
Returned-task list remains empty in the live environment, so continued pre-resubmit visibility cannot be observed.
No returned task detail can be opened from the live UI, so latest return reason/node rendering cannot be confirmed through the user surface.

Realtime normalization behavior is only covered by automated tests right now; no live websocket-delivered returned-task update can be observed because the environment lacks suitable returned-task data.

appliedUpdates.description: Added browser-ui flow validator guidance for serial validation, surface boundaries, and how to treat missing returned-task seed data as an environment blocker.
